input.headline: In times of sweeping change, clarity matters most.

raw.headline: In times of sweeping change, clarity matters most.

winner: In times of sweeping change, clarity matters most — especially when your spouse is rebranding the bathroom as "The Zen Den.

candidates.text: Why did the compass go into therapy? It had too much *change* and needed some *clarity*.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'change' and 'clarity' align with the headline, are concise, surprising, and specific."}

candidates.text: Why did the compass break up with the GPS during the digital revolution? It said, "I need some *sweeping* clarity in this relationship—you're just too *changeable* for me!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative use of the headline's keywords with a clever twist on technology metaphors; concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: I told my GPS we're going through a time of sweeping change, so it better sweep me straight to the bathroom.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Smart wordplay on 'sweeping change' and GPS functionality, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: In times of sweeping change, clarity matters most — especially when your spouse is rebranding the bathroom as "The Zen Den.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific, with a relatable twist on rebranding in a domestic setting."}

candidates.text: In times of sweeping change, clarity matters most, which is why I’ve decided to stop trying to understand my taxes and start just staring at the paper until it makes sense.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is relatable and specific, using a common frustration (taxes) to highlight the headline's message. It’s slightly generic in its approach but still effective with a touch of absurdity."}

candidates.text: In times of sweeping change, clarity matters most — which is why I always bring a flashlight to family dinners.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and uses a surprising twist (flashlight at family dinners). It ties back to the headline but could be more impactful with greater absurdity or deeper connection."}

candidates.text: When the government announced that clarity matters most during times of change, they replaced all city street signs with giant, high-powered spotlights and a megaphone that just screams "IT'S A ROAD" at everyone simultaneously.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.2, "reason": "Surprising and specific take on the headline with a humorous overreaction to clarity."}

candidates.text: When the world is in a state of constant flux, it’s hard to find your footing, but I’m just trying to make sure my vision stays crystal clear—I’m a professional glass blower.
I tried to explain my plans for world peace during the chaos of the robot uprising, but they just kept asking for a "clearer" version in 140 characters.
